{{Quote|Unfortunately, ''[Lyechusas']'' original manuscripts have not weathered the travails of time as well as the molt that hangs in Argai's Royal Theatre and is said to be hers.|Professor S. V. Skynx, in ''Ronton's Anthology to Galactic Literature'', Vol. 2|<ref name="XimII">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek03/index.html|text=Xim Week: ''The Despotica'' (Part II: The Pirate Prince)|int=Xim Week: The Despotica}}</ref>}}
The '''Lisst'n''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that continually shed their skin. They were active in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] dating from the [[Time/Legends|time]] of [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]], circa [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]. According to legend, [[Lyechusas]], Xim's court [[Poet/Legends|poet]] and the most renowned Lisst'n [[Author/Legends|dramatist]], penned three trilogies on the life of the Despot from this time. In the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[University of Rudrig/Legends|University of Rudrig]], the sole [[University/Legends|university]] in the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]], had several luxury [[dermal autostripper]] services available for members of the Lisst'n species.

==Biology and appearance==
The Lisst'n were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that continually shed their skin.<ref name="CSWE" /> During the period of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Lisst'n utilized [[dermal autostripper]]s to manage this molting.<ref name="HLL">''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]''</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|Lyechusas never shrinks from exposing the dark side of her Despot: she imbues Xim with all the ruthlessness and arrogance later dramatists will caricature to staggering exaggeration.|Professor S. V. Skynx, in ''Ronton's Anthology to Galactic Literature'', Vol. 2|<ref name="XimII" />}}
The Lisst'n were present in the [[galactic community]] from as early as [[25,200 BBY]], when [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]] ruled an [[Xim's empire|interstellar empire]]. According to legend, Xim commissioned [[Lyechusas]], his court [[Poet/Legends|poet]] and the most renowned of Lisst'n [[Author/Legends|dramatists]], to script three trilogies on his life; however, by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] some doubt had risen over the authenticity of this legend after [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Ransen]], a scholar of [[Ximology]], proposed that the [[The Despotica|original literature]] surrounding Xim could instead have originated in [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] propaganda. Other historians, such as [[S. V. Skynx/Legends|S. V. Skynx]], disputed Ransen's hypothesis. While Lyechusas' original manuscripts perished over time, fragments of her works survived for over 25,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] in [[Oor VII|Oor]] translations. The [[Royal Theatre]] of [[Argai]], where Lyechusas had hailed from, claimed to hold an authentic molt of her skin.<ref name="XimII" /> The [[Argai Players]] annually performed her trilogies to a sold-out audience.<ref name="Xim Week: The Despotica">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek02/index.html|text=Xim Week: ''The Despotica'' (Part I)|int=Xim Week: The Despotica}}</ref> One such script of Lyechusas was entitled ''[[The Pirate Prince]]''.<ref name="XimII" />

During the [[2 BBY/Legends|latter years]] of the Galactic Empire, the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony's]] sole [[University/Legends|university]], the [[University of Rudrig/Legends|University of Rudrig]], had a number of luxury dermal autostripper services available for Lisst'n, as well as for members of the [[Pui-ui]] species.<ref name="HLL" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Lisst'n were created by [[Brian Daley]] for his novel ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'', published by [[Del Rey]] in [[1980]]. This brief mention revealed little about the species. They later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]]. In [[2009]], [[Michael Kogge]] first named the Lisst'n in his [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]-exclusive article ''[[Xim Week: The Despotica]]''.
